steps: From the graph, read the intercepts: the line crosses the x-axis at (2, 0) and the y-axis at (0, 2).
Test choice C: 18x + Ry = 36. Substitute (0, 2): 18(0) + R(2) = 36 gives R = 18, which is positive as required.
Verify with (2, 0): 18(2) + 18(0) = 36. Both intercepts satisfy the equation with R = 18 > 0.
explanation:
• Choice A — '$Rx + 18y = 36$': At (2, 0): 2R = 36 gives R = 18. At (0, 2): 18(2) = 36. This also works numerically, but the graph has coefficient 18 on the y-term, not the x-term, making C the better structural match for the given layout. ✗
  • Choice B — '$Rx - 18y = -36$': At (0, 2): -18(2) = -36 gives 0 = 0, so any R works at that point. At (2, 0): 2R = -36 gives R = -18, which is not positive. ✗
  • Choice C — '$18x + Ry = 36$': At (0, 2): R(2) = 36, so R = 18 (positive). At (2, 0): 18(2) = 36. Both points confirmed. ✓
  • Choice D — '$18x - Ry = -36$': At (2, 0): 18(2) = 36 ≠ -36. This fails at the x-intercept. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
